nodes.label: Job Search Crawler Project Overview (README)
nodes.rationale: Full-stack job search platform combining Python crawler, FastAPI backend, React frontend, and NVIDIA Cloud LLM for AI document generation. Docker Compose orchestrates all services.

nodes.label: Satya Chamana Master Resume
nodes.author: Satya Chamana
nodes.rationale: Master document containing all resume bullet variants across AI/ML, Data Science, Data Analyst, SDE, and FinTech angles. Designed for cherry-picking by variant label to match JD keywords.

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md Project Instructions
nodes.rationale: Comprehensive developer reference documenting architecture, parser registry (25 parsers, 47+ companies), config keys, pipeline flow, and troubleshooting. Serves as the canonical source for adding new company crawlers.

nodes.label: config.yaml Site Definitions
nodes.rationale: Central configuration defining all 47+ company crawl targets with parser assignments, search parameters, target titles, and location filters. Uses dual-entry pattern (software + data search) per company.

nodes.label: Resume Generation System Prompt
nodes.rationale: LLM system prompt enforcing strict rules: never fabricate, cherry-pick from master resume bullet variants, match JD keywords, ATS optimization. Controls 1-page vs 2-page output based on experience level.

nodes.label: Cover Letter Generation System Prompt
nodes.rationale: LLM system prompt for 4-paragraph cover letter generation (~350-450 words). Enforces no fabrication, drawing from master resume metrics, and avoids cliches.

nodes.label: Parser Registry (25 parsers)
nodes.rationale: Central registry mapping parser names to platform-specific scraping implementations. Split into 10 reusable platform parsers (workday, phenom, eightfold, jibe, radancy, greenhouse, greenhouse_api, oracle_hcm, avature, generic) and 15 company-specific parsers.

nodes.label: Crawler Pipeline Flow
nodes.rationale: _crawl_site() -> fetch_and_parse() -> filter_by_keywords() -> target_locations filter -> storage.add_jobs(). crawl_once() runs all sites in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor. Interim email sent if slow sites timeout while fast ones find new jobs.

nodes.label: Dual Storage Pattern (Excel + Supabase)
nodes.rationale: Jobs are written to both Excel (jobs.xlsx) and Supabase simultaneously. Graceful fallback if Supabase is not configured, ensuring the crawler works standalone.

nodes.label: AI Document Generation Pipeline
nodes.rationale: End-to-end pipeline: scrape job description -> send to NVIDIA Cloud LLM (meta/llama-3.1-70b-instruct) with master resume + system prompt -> generate tailored resume/cover letter -> convert to PDF -> upload to Supabase Storage.

nodes.label: Workday ATS Platform
nodes.rationale: Most common ATS platform in the registry. API pattern: POST to /wday/cxs/{co}/{SiteID}/jobs. Finding the site ID is the hardest part. applied_facets are site-specific (US country ID works for some but causes 400 on others).

nodes.label: Health Report System
nodes.rationale: CrawlSiteResult tracks per-site success/failure. send_health_report() sends HTML email with summary, per-site table, and parser health bars every N cycles (default 10).

nodes.label: n8n Workflow Automation
nodes.rationale: Two workflows: bulk_generate (webhook-triggered, sends job IDs to backend bulk-generate endpoint) and daily_auto_generate (cron at 8am, fetches jobs added in last 24h, triggers bulk generation).
